chunk_summary: Micronutrient supplementation and infection prevention are critical adjuncts in managing wasting, emphasizing public health programs like Anaemia Mukt Bharat, deworming, Vitamin A prophylaxis, and zinc supplementation for diarrhoea management.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of micronutrient supplementation in managing wasting and preventing infections.
Learn the prophylactic iron–folic acid (IFA) supplementation schedule under Anaemia Mukt Bharat for different age groups.
Recognize the importance of deworming, Vitamin A prophylaxis, and zinc supplementation in outpatient care for malnourished children.
key_facts: Anaemia Mukt Bharat provides IFA syrup for ages 6–59 months (1 mL twice weekly, containing 20 mg iron and 100 mcg folic acid).
Albendazole is administered as a single 200 mg dose for ages 1–2 years and 400 mg for ages 2–19 years during National Deworming Day campaigns.
Vitamin A prophylaxis includes 100,000 IU at 9 months and 200,000 IU every 6 months up to 5 years.
Zinc supplementation for acute diarrhoea is 20 mg daily for 10–14 days, or 10 mg daily for infants under 6 months.
Routine outpatient visits should verify immunization status and promote infection prevention measures like handwashing and safe water.
reference_quote: Anaemia Mukt Bharat provides prophylactic iron–folic acid (IFA) supplementation as syrup for ages 6–59 months at 1 mL twice weekly, containing 20 mg elemental iron and 100 mcg folic acid.
